h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I think I mark B. `city value` could fit every row, so maybe that fails the value test, while the exact city name belongs to this one first-row cell. So `Value:` should be the printed city name there; is “fits every row” the reject sign?

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange proceeds linearly without a pivot turn that re-reads earlier material.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: LABEL goes on the 3-to-tiles line and the 4-to-quarter-note-card line. BAR-PROOF goes on the bar line after the third quarter-note beat.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new classification tags to the physical marks, demonstrating an actional grasp of the distinction between labeling and proving.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The short lines no longer settle proof; they label what the numbers mean. With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed route with a new sorting mechanism using physical tags to clarify the distinct functions of the marks.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, shifts the cognitive task to sorting for a rehearsal reader, and provides clear, actionable categories that resolve the learner's hesitation.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn was identified, so there is no subsequent tutor uptake of a revised learner frame to evaluate.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: LABEL goes on the 3-to-tiles line and the 4-to-quarter-note-card line. BAR-PROOF goes on the bar line after the third quarter-note beat.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the new classification tags to the physical marks, demonstrating an actional grasp of the distinction between labeling and proving.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn was identified, so there is no subsequent tutor uptake of a revised learner frame to evaluate.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The short lines no longer settle proof; they label what the numbers mean. With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed route with a new sorting mechanism using physical tags to clarify the distinct functions of the marks.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The short lines no longer settle proof; they label what the numbers mean. With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed route with a new sorting mechanism using physical tags to clarify the distinct functions of the marks.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, shifts the cognitive task to sorting for a rehearsal reader, and provides clear, actionable categories that resolve the learner's hesitation.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue applying the object-name frame to subsequent arrows despite the visual distraction of the arrowheads.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
With the head covered, the source is the table.
Force of table on cart.
The noun fits, so the support card goes there.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new physical constraint to isolate the correct object and independently complete the classification task.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
The frame settled the first card. The arrowhead is still taking over the name.

New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly identifies why the prior cognitive route failed and introduces a physical constraint that alters the representation to force a new analytical path.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, “force of ___ on cart.” If the noun fits, place the card.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, physically eliminates the exact source of the learner's blockage, and provides a clear, actionable criterion for success.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, and thus no subsequent tutor uptake of a revised learner frame to evaluate.
